problem_statement: The Agentic Service Harness readiness map classifies RepositoryConnection as partial because the read model does not yet carry durable provider repository identity, installation state, effective permission scopes, revocation evidence, and verification timestamp needed before any user-facing harness or adapter path can depend on it.
success_criteria: RepositoryConnection source contract and read model require provider repository refs, repository owner/name, installation ref and state, effective read permission scopes, revocation state/evidence, last verification timestamp, credential binding ref, false secret serialization, and false write authority
Projection from harness contract fixtures to read models preserves the durable repository connection fields without hand-authored drift
Validators reject missing repository refs, invalid installation or revocation state, serialized secret values, enabled write authority, and write-like effective permission scopes

Default examples remain Foundation Mode, read-only, non-mutating, and free of dashboard, mutation endpoint, external adapter, branch creation, pull-request creation, deployment, DNS, secret, or destructive authority
Focused contract, read-model, projection, persistence, integrity, schema, and SDLC validators pass
constraints: RepositoryConnection is read-model and contract hardening only
Effective permission scopes remain read-only even for approval-gated branch-write and open-PR planning scenarios
Revocation status is evidence-linked and does not authorize provider mutation
Write authority remains false in schemas, examples, projection, validators, and tests
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved because no fidel processing is introduced
